business_definition: A Vellum-native deterministic rule definition stored as JSON and evaluated against canonical contract objects.
design_principles: Rules are versioned data artifacts, not hidden code paths.
Rules evaluate canonical contracts, not provider-specific payloads.
LLMs may assist with explanation and enrichment but do not override deterministic outcomes.

fields.rule_family: Logical grouping such as custody, reconciliation, workflow, or approvals.
fields.status: Lifecycle state of the rule definition.
fields.description: Plain-language description of what the rule checks.
fields.determinism_class: Classification of the rule evaluation mode; currently deterministic only.
fields.target_contract_types: Canonical contract types the rule is designed to evaluate.
fields.input_scope: How records are grouped, filtered, joined, or windowed before rule evaluation.
fields.expression_language: JSON-based expression language used by the predicate.
fields.predicate: The JSON expression that evaluates to true/false or equivalent deterministic output.
fields.severity: Default severity classification if the rule is triggered.
fields.materiality: Optional logic for static or expression-based materiality classification.
fields.outcome: The deterministic result and workflow/control actions if the rule is triggered.
fields.evidence: Fields and optional RAG collections used for explanation and operator context.
fields.workflow_binding: Optional routing metadata into workflow cases and approvals.
fields.test_cases: Minimal built-in expectation metadata for rule test scenarios.
fields.tags: Free-form rule tags for discovery and governance.
